# Check what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']

for label, seg in [("BEFORE", before), ("AFTER", after)]:

            rows = [p[0] for p in b]
            cols = [p[1] for p in b]
            print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

When I moved the charcoal right, it went from cols 10-13 to cols 22-25. The right boundary seems to be around col 25-26. Let me check if there's a barrier there.

Actually, the charcoal is at rows 52-57, which is below the black bar at rows 40-43. The black bar shouldn't directly block the charcoal's horizontal movement.
